_protocol: Calibration topic — Strait of Hormuz closure duration forecast. Original flagship topic, tracking since 2026-03-02. 124 evidence entries. Ceasefire agreed Apr 8, collapsed Apr 11. Islamabad talks failed. Trump announced US naval blockade Apr 12. Now dual blockade (Iran + US). Mine-clearing ops underway.

meta.title: HORMUZ STRAIT CLOSURE
meta.question: How long will the Strait of Hormuz remain effectively closed?
meta.resolution: Sustained >30% of pre-war Hormuz traffic (~40 transits/day)

tagConfig._docs: Tag configuration for evidence classification and R_t scoring.

posteriorHistory.note: RESTORE: topic restored from archive at 2026-05-03T20:36:39.280807+00:00. Prior posteriorHistory discarded (contaminated by pre-gate freeform-LR loophole). Posteriors reset to design priors. evidenceLog dropped, governance recomputed, sourceCalibration reset. Indicator schema preserved with shape='single_observation' default.

khargTimeline.assaultWindow: ACTIVE — initial ops underway as of Apr 7
khargTimeline.note: EVOLVED SIGNIFICANTLY: Tripoli offshore Kharg running F-35B SEAD missions. SEAL/Raider 'Quiet Entry' ops on southern tip reported. 50+ military targets struck Apr 7. IRGC claims missile hit on Tripoli (unconfirmed). Boxer ARG arrived in theater. 50,000+ US troops now in region. Trump gave 'Execute' order for initial phase per some reports. Simultaneously, mine-clearing ops launched in Hormuz (USS Peterson + USS Murphy + UUVs). Mission is now multi-track: Kharg seizure + Hormuz clearing + blockade enforcement.

trumpUltimatum.note: RESOLVED: Deadline extended 3x (Mar 23→28→Apr 6→Apr 7), then suspended for 2-week ceasefire (Apr 8). Ceasefire collapsed after 21hr Islamabad talks (Apr 11). Trump pivoted to naval blockade (Apr 12) + Kharg military strikes (Apr 7) + mine clearing (Apr 11). Outcome = hybrid of D (extended into ceasefire) then C (pivoted to ground/maritime ops after talks failed). Power plants never hit. Submodel effectively resolved — Trump's ultimatum cycle ended with blockade announcement.

direct_talks_resume.label: Direct talks resume after cooling period

maximalist_deadlock.label: Both sides' positions too far apart, talks collapse

talksTrack.note: RESOLVED PHASE 1: Indirect relay channel escalated to face-to-face talks in Islamabad (Apr 11). Vance/Witkoff/Kushner met Iranian delegation — first since 2015. 21hr marathon. COLLAPSED. Irreconcilable sticking points: (1) Iran nuclear enrichment — US demands halt, Iran refuses, (2) Hormuz tolls — US demands free passage, Iran claims sovereignty right, (3) Lebanon — Iran demands Israel stop Hezbollah strikes, US says excluded. Trump pivoted to blockade within hours of collapse. Channel exists (Pakistan mediation) but positions too far apart for near-term deal. Direct talks now 0.30 — Islamabad established the precedent of face-to-face, parties could return after pressure cycle. But maximalist_deadlock dominant at 0.55.

tier1_critical.desc: NOTAM/NAVAREA warning for northern Persian Gulf / Kharg vicinity
Tripoli ARG AIS goes dark in Gulf of Oman for 24+ hours
tier1_critical.note: Tripoli ENTERED CENTCOM AOR Fri Mar 27. Now in transit to Gulf â€” 'several additional days' to reach Hormuz. AIS dark watch starts NOW. Can go dark anytime between now and Gulf of Oman arrival.
REPLAY: FIRED: Multiple new phases — US struck 50+ Kharg military targets Apr 7, CENTCOM

tier1_critical.desc: Massive uptick in airstrikes on Bushehr province / northern Gulf coast
tier1_critical.note: REPLAY: FIRED: Apr 7 US struck 50+ military targets on Kharg Island (Bushehr province).

tier2_strong.desc: Tripoli tracked entering Persian Gulf (not Gulf of Oman)
tier2_strong.note: REPLAY: FIRED: USS Tripoli positioned offshore Kharg Island. F-35Bs conducting SEAD miss

tier2_strong.desc: Trump goes quiet on Kharg for 48+ hours (OPSEC)
tier2_strong.note: Trump currently talking about 'talks' â€” not quiet. Check if Kharg drops from rhetoric.

tier2_strong.desc: Pentagon briefings suddenly vague about MEU mission
Evacuation orders for US civilians in Gulf states
tier2_strong.note: Iran struck Kuwait (airport fuel tank Mar 25), Bahrain, UAE, Saudi (32 drones + 1 BM intercepted in 11h), Jordan. Evac orders increasingly plausible.

tier2_strong.desc: 48hr ultimatum expires without power plant strikes (Kharg substituted)
tier2_strong.note: REPLAY: CONFIRMED: Trump postponed strikes Mon Mar 23, extended deadline 5 days. Did NOT

tier2_strong.desc: 82nd Airborne or other ground forces deploying to theater
tier2_strong.note: REPLAY: UPGRADED x2: ~3,000 troops from 82nd Airborne (Al Jazeera Day 26) including divi

tier2_strong.desc: Named DoD/Navy official or Congressional testimony stating mine-clearance operations require >=6 months from commencement of clearing. Captures the post-conflict clearance tail as a duration component separable from conflict duration. Observable threshold: publicly reported testimony/briefing from a named official, corroborated by at least one independent outlet.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H3->H4 mass shift (~5-10pp from H3, 3-5pp from H2/H1 residual to H4). Reflects that if clearance alone takes >=26 weeks and can only begin after hostilities cease, any remaining-conflict duration >0 pushes total toward H4. Apply once per distinct testimony event; do not re-fire on restatements of the same underlying DoD estimate.
tier2_strong.design_note: Added 2026-04-24 to close indicator-tier gap: clearance-duration testimony was previously uncategorized and being logged as impact=NONE (see ev_173). Closes blind spot flagged by governance alert on this topic.
tier2_strong.note: FIRED via canvas action button. Testimony that Hormuz mine clearance requires ~6 months captures post-conflict clearance tail as a duration component independent of conflict duration. Evidence: ev_173 (US Navy officials to Congress, Apr 2026).

tier2_strong.desc: US naval blockade of Iranian ports remains in force >=30 consecutive days alongside Hormuz closure
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H1 down near-zero; H3 +10-15pp; H4 +3-5pp. Mutual blockade structurally extends closure timeline; symmetric escalation has no rapid off-ramp.

tier2_strong.desc: Sustained Hormuz transit volume <15% of pre-war baseline (<=15 transits/day rolling 21-day window)
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H1 collapse to ~0; H3 +8-12pp. Quantitative ratification that brief-resolution scenarios are mechanically dead.

tier2_strong.desc: 3+ US aircraft carrier strike groups concurrently in CENTCOM AOR for >=7 days
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H3 +5-8pp; H4 +2-3pp. 3-carrier surge signals indefinite naval commitment; deployment cycles imply months not weeks.

tier3_suggestive.desc: More 'agonizing decision' articles with operational specifics
tier3_suggestive.note: REPLAY: CNN Mar 21 deep dive matches pre-invasion socialization pattern exactly.
tier3_suggestive.lr_source: historical analogy: pre-2003-Iraq / pre-2011-Libya public socialization

tier3_suggestive.note: Senator referenced 'bloodiest battle in USMC history' while urging Kharg attack (Military.com Mar 23). Congressional advocacy, not briefing.
tier3_suggestive.lr_source: briefings precede kinetic action days-weeks (Soleimani, OBL raid)

tier3_suggestive.desc: Defense stocks spike / oil spikes on no new news
tier3_suggestive.note: Oil crashed 11% Mon on 'talks' then recovered Tue. Volatility driven by news, not smart money.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Israel increases strike tempo as coordinated distraction
tier3_suggestive.note: REPLAY: Katz announced significant increase next week. IDF Chief approved 'all fronts' s

tier3_suggestive.desc: CENTCOM specifically targeting Hormuz coastal defense assets
tier3_suggestive.note: REPLAY: Cooper confirmed 5000lb bombs on underground coastal anti-ship missile facility.
tier3_suggestive.lr_source: CENTCOM pre-op AShM-suppression; Praying Mantis 1988, 2003 ops

tier3_suggestive.desc: Iran strikes US-allied Gulf state territory (not just Israel)
tier3_suggestive.note: IRGC seized MSC Francesca and Greek-owned Epaminondas in Strait of Hormuz on 2026-04-22; Iranian gun

tier3_suggestive.desc: Iran formalizes blockade terms at international body
tier3_suggestive.note: Trump on 2026-04-22 extended US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely while keeping US naval blockade of Irani

tier3_suggestive.desc: Iran/IRGC seizes or fires on >=3 commercial vessels within 14-day window during closure period
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H3 +3-5pp. Demonstrated Iranian enforcement capacity and political will to maintain closure.

tier3_suggestive.desc: 2+ named major-energy or petrochem CEOs publicly forecast Hormuz reopening only in H2 2026 or later

anti_indicators.desc: Iran formally proposes reopening Hormuz contingent on a US/coalition action (sanctions relief, blockade lift, ceasefire). Confirmed by ≥2 independent Tier-1 sources.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: Shifts mass toward H2 (6wk-4mo). LRs: H1 0.7, H2 0.7, H3 0.5, H4 0.2.
anti_indicators.note: Iran formally delivered 14-point proposal to US (via Pakistani mediators) including reopen of Strait

anti_indicators.desc: US/coalition publicly signals willingness to ease the naval blockade or accept a negotiated reopening. Confirmed by official statement (CENTCOM, State, WH, or NSC) or ≥2 reliable secondary sources.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: Shifts mass toward H2/H1. LRs: H1 0.7, H2 0.75, H3 0.4, H4 0.2.

anti_indicators.desc: Quantitative confirmation Iran's onshore crude storage is <7 days remaining. Threshold: government admission, satellite tank-farm imagery analysis (e.g., Vortexa, Kpler, OilX), or official oil-export-union statement specifying days-remaining figure.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: Forces near-term reopening pressure. LRs: H1 0.9, H2 0.9, H3 0.4, H4 0.15.

anti_indicators.desc: Hormuz transits sustained at ≥10 ships/day for 7 consecutive days (one-quarter of the resolution criterion of ≥30%/~40 tpd). Source: Lloyd's List, MarineTraffic, or USNI proxies.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: Strong reopen signal, near partial resolution. LRs: H1 0.95, H2 0.9, H3 0.3, H4 0.1.

anti_indicators.desc: Kharg Island crude loadings resume at ≥30% of pre-war daily rate, sustained 7 days. Source: Vortexa/Kpler tanker tracking or NIOC official statement.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: Direct reopen evidence on Iran's primary export node. LRs: H1 0.95, H2 0.9, H3 0.3, H4 0.1.

actorModel.description: Trump-adjusted decision framework with four layers

trump.name: President Trump
trump.decisionStyle: Impulsive, fixation-driven, spectacle-optimizing. Evaluates operations on immediate visual results, not long-term strategic cost.
biases: Claims credit for things not happening (fake 'talks')
filters: Military planning process filters novel stupidity
overrides: Personal fixations bypass tactical filter â€” Kharg qualifies because assault will succeed on Day 1

centcom.name: CENTCOM / Joint Staff
centcom.decisionStyle: Competent tactical execution within parameters given. 9,000+ combat flights, 9,000+ targets hit.
filters: Normal planning process filters tactically idiotic ideas
overrides: Direct presidential order gets executed regardless of strategic risk assessment

iran.name: Iranian leadership (IRGC + civilian)
iran.role: Adversary â€” controls Hormuz closure and escalation pace
iran.decisionStyle: Attrition-oriented, escalatory after nuclear site strikes. NOW: blocked Chinese ships (corridor dead), IRGC warned civilians to evacuate near US bases (pre-attack signal), vows retaliation 'no longer eye for eye.' IRGC consolidating power: Zolghadr at SNSC, Tangsiri dead with no replacement = C2 void in IRGC Navy. Mojtaba Khamenei still not seen. 2/3 of missiles intact â€” can sustain attacks for months.
overrides: Counter-threat on power plants appears to have deterred Trump â€” first successful Iranian deterrence in this conflict

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC â€” only kinetic events, force movements, budget requests, verified data move posteriors. Trump words discounted. IRGC threats tagged THREATENED until executed.
SOCIALIZATION PATTERN RECOGNITION â€” 'agonizing decision' articles with hyper-specific operational detail = narrative prep for decision already on conveyor belt
DON'T FRONT-RUN â€” set tripwires and let news confirm. Posteriors move when indicators fire, not when thesis feels good.
TRUMP FIXATION OVERRIDE â€” when CinC has personal fixation predating the situation, tactical filter is bypassed. Kharg = 38yr fixation.
TRUMP RHETORIC DISCOUNT â€” 'talks' claim assessed as face-saving for ultimatum blink. Iran denied. Mediator relay confirmed but is not negotiation. DO NOT move posteriors on Trump words alone.

evidenceLog.text: DEADLINE ELIMINATION: hypothesis H1 past resolution_deadline (2026-04-13). Mass redistributed to H2, H3, H4.
US claims Hormuz blockade continues, ~20,000 seafarers stranded
UAE intercepted Iranian missiles first time since ceasefire
US Navy guiding ships through Hormuz as workaround
Iran says Hormuz is open but controls transit

brent.label: Brent Crude
brent.unit: $/bbl (intraday high Apr 15; range $94.66-96.80, volatile. EIA raised 2026 projection to $96)

hormuz_traffic.label: Hormuz Traffic
hormuz_traffic.unit: % of pre-war (~10 transits/day during ceasefire vs 120 pre-war; 3 supertankers Apr 11)

transits_day.label: Transits/Day
transits_day.unit: ships (ceasefire-period avg; up from 5 pre-ceasefire but still 92% below normal. US blockade may reduce back to near-zero)

iran_dead.label: Iran Dead
iran_dead.unit: (last confirmed pre-ceasefire; likely higher after Apr 7 Kharg strikes)

trump_approval.label: Trump Approval
trump_approval.unit: % (last confirmed; Pew: 59% say wrong to use force)

watchpoints.event: US naval blockade implementation - does Navy actually intercept ships?
watchpoints.watch: Trump announced blockade via Truth Social Apr 12. Key: (1) Does Navy physically intercept commercial vessels? (2) Which ships - all, or just Iran-bound? (3) Does this apply to Chinese/Indian tankers that previously paid Iran toll? First enforcement action sets precedent. If US intercepts a Chinese vessel = major escalation with Beijing. IRGC vowed to retaliate against military vessels. Watch for first confrontation.

watchpoints.event: Mine-clearing progress - USS Peterson + Murphy + UUVs
watchpoints.watch: CENTCOM launched mine clearance Apr 11. UUV drones scanning for Maham 3 (moored) and Maham 7 (bottom) mines. Additional forces joining in coming days. UK/France minesweeping coalition forming. Iran threatened to target mine-clearing ships. Key: (1) How many mines found vs estimated? (2) Any new mines laid after clearing starts? (3) Timeline to certify safe commercial lanes - typically weeks to months. (4) IRGC attacks on clearing ships = massive escalation.

Reports of SEAL/Raider Quiet Entry on southern tip. Tripoli F-35Bs running SEAD. 50+ military targets struck Apr 7. IRGC claims missile hit on Tripoli (unconfirmed). Boxer ARG now in theater. 50,000 troops total. Is this active seizure, prep for seizure, or holding pattern during blockade? Watch: (1) Pentagon announces ground operation, (2) AIS data on Tripoli/Boxer positions, (3) Iran reports ground combat on Kharg.

watchpoints.event: Market reaction to blockade announcement (Monday open)
watchpoints.watch: Brent at 95.20 pre-announcement. Blockade announced Sunday - markets closed. Monday open will price in dual blockade + failed talks. Expect significant spike. If Brent breaks 120 = new regime. Oil market reaction signals how long traders expect closure to last.

watchpoints.event: Ceasefire status - does 2-week truce survive or formally collapse?
watchpoints.watch: Ceasefire agreed Apr 8 for 2 weeks (expires ~Apr 22). Talks collapsed Apr 11 but ceasefire not formally terminated. Trump announced blockade but not resumption of bombing. Iran has not declared ceasefire over. Ambiguous state: (1) If both sides resume strikes = ceasefire dead, war enters new phase, (2) If blockade coexists with ceasefire = strange limbo, pressure without kinetic escalation. Watch Iran next move.

watchpoints.event: Houthi Red Sea shipping decision - STILL the H4 swing factor
watchpoints.watch: Houthis hitting Israel but NOT Red Sea shipping. Strategic restraint so far. If Houthis activate Bab al-Mandab chokepoint = dual maritime crisis, Cape route overloaded, H4 surges to 0.45+. Operation Rough Rider (US strikes on Yemen) may have deterred maritime attacks. Watch: any Houthi statements about shipping, Red Sea incidents, or US force redeployment to Bab al-Mandab.

Resolution criterion (40 transits/day) now requires: (1) Iran stands down blockade, (2) US stands down counter-blockade, (3) Mines cleared from strait, (4) Insurance companies willing to cover transits, (5) 600+ stranded ships processed through. Each prerequisite adds weeks-months. Even optimistic scenario = months before normal traffic resumes. This is why H4 surged.

watchpoints.event: Islamabad channel - does Pakistan try round 2?
watchpoints.watch: 21hr talks failed but channel exists. Pakistan PM Sharif invested in mediator role. Face-to-face precedent established. After pressure from blockade, one or both sides may return. Watch: Pakistan diplomatic signals, back-channel reports, any new proposals. If no talks resume within 2 weeks = deadlock hardens.

watchpoints.event: Iran toll regime vs US interdiction - sovereignty clash
watchpoints.watch: Iran claims sovereignty right to toll ships. US says free passage non-negotiable. This was a collapse point in Islamabad talks. Now being tested physically - US intercepting toll-paying ships. If US successfully prevents all toll payments, Iran loses economic lifeline from blockade. If Iran retaliates against US enforcement = kinetic escalation at sea.

issues: Stale dependency: calibration-houthi-posture.H2 assumed=0.5665, actual=0.9684, drift=40.19%
Stale dependency: calibration-houthi-posture.H3 assumed=0.3221, actual=0.0216, drift=30.05%

warnings: meta.resolution may not be objectively observable — does it describe something a third party could verify?

dependencies._docs: Cross-topic dependency declarations. Upstream topics whose posteriors this topic assumptions depend on.

upstream.reason: Houthi posture in the Red Sea/Bab al-Mandeb directly affects alternative shipping routes around the Horn of Africa. If Houthis de-escalate (H1), pressure to reopen Hormuz increases as the alternative route becomes viable. If Houthis maintain or escalate, the dual chokepoint closure extends Hormuz closure duration.
upstream.driftNote: Synced 2026-04-24. H2 drifted ++0.117 (0.45 -> 0.5665) exceeding tolerance 0.10. Selective-targeting posture has consolidated as the dominant Houthi mode; both escalation tails (H1 -0.046, H4 -0.092) contracted, H3 ceasefire +0.022. Causal link to Hormuz still holds: Red Sea remains partially restricted for Israeli/US-linked shipping, so the Horn-of-Africa alternative is only partially viable. Net effect on Hormuz is roughly neutral vs prior sync (H4 dual-blockade tail shrank, but H2 residual friction grew). Assumption retained; values refreshed to current upstream posteriors.

predictionScoring._docs: Prediction scoring and calibration tracking. Snapshots are taken on each posterior update. Outcomes recorded at resolution.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: Oil prices remain above USD 85/barrel through Q3 2026
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Brent crude monthly average for Jul/Aug/Sep 2026 all above USD 85

conditionalPredictions.prediction: Houthi Red Sea posture shifts to selective targeting (H2) by July 2026
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: calibration-houthi-posture leading hypothesis is H2 on 2026-07-01

conditionalPredictions.prediction: If Hormuz reopens within 4 months, Brent drops below USD 75 by August
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Brent crude monthly average for Aug 2026 below USD 75

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF 4-12 month closure, THEN LNG spot prices in NW Europe exceed $35/MMBtu on ≥3 separate trading days by 2026-09-30
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Bloomberg or Platts shows ≥3 daily NW Europe LNG prices >$35/MMBtu between Apr 18-Sep 30 2026

AMBER.reasoning: $35/MMBtu LNG breach captures a price-regime discontinuity in NW Europe gas.

BLUE.reasoning: $35/MMBtu on 3+ days overstates breakdown; EU gas storage policy, reshuffled LNG cargoes, and demand response are robust absorption mechanisms that cap spikes.

RED.reasoning: LNG spot >$35/MMBtu on 3+ days under 4-12mo Hormuz closure is a clean energy-shock indicator. RED-aligned.

VIOLET.reasoning: Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H3, THEN insurance premium for VLCCs on Cape route exceeds 3.0% of hull value by 2026-08-15
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: ICS or Lloyd's published VLCC war risk premium >3.0% on at least one publication date between May 1-Aug 15 2026

AMBER.reasoning: >3% hull-value war-risk premium is a threshold-crossing insurance-regime signal.

BLUE.reasoning: 3.0% hull-value premium is plausibly high but under-specifies whether the Cape rerouting itself clears the risk. Reframe to include that premiums settle at a lower equilibrium once Cape rerouting is underwritten as standard.
BLUE.modified_suggestion: IF H3, THEN VLCC war-risk premium spikes above 3.0% of hull value at least once between May 1-Aug 15 2026, then settles into a stable Cape-route premium of 1.0-2.0% by 2026-09-30 as underwriters normalize the reroute.

RED.reasoning: VLCC war-risk premium >3.0% hull value is a sharp brittleness signal. Good.

VIOLET.reasoning: Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H3 (4-12 month closure), THEN ≥1 OPEC+ member outside Gulf reduces crude output by ≥2 Mb/d cumulatively over closure window due to sanctions/credit constraints cascading from energy price volatility
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: IEA or EIA monthly data shows ≥2 Mb/d aggregate reduction from Feb 2026 baseline for named non-Gulf OPEC+ cohort (Angola, Iraq, Nigeria, Russia, or Kazakhstan) with causal attribution to financial or sanctions pressure by Dec 31 2026

AMBER.reasoning: 2 Mb/d non-Gulf OPEC+ cut from sanctions/credit cascade is a clear cross-regime contagion signal.

BLUE.reasoning: 2 Mb/d non-Gulf OPEC+ reduction from financial contagion overstates cascade risk; OPEC+ coordination and Gulf spare capacity are the canonical absorption mechanisms.

GRAY.reasoning: 'Causal attribution to financial pressure' is hard to establish; reframe.
GRAY.modified_suggestion: IF H3, THEN 2+ Mb/d aggregate output reduction from named non-Gulf OPEC+ states during Jun-Dec 2026, with 2+ peer-reviewed or IEA analyses citing financial-sanction mechanism.

RED.reasoning: Non-Gulf OPEC+ member output reduction via sanctions/credit contagion is a clean second-order channel. Good.

VIOLET.reasoning: Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H3, THEN structural refinery utilization in East Asia (China, India, Japan, South Korea combined) declines to <85% for ≥1 month by Oct 2026 due to demand destruction from elevated product prices
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: IEA or joint national statistical agency data shows combined refinery utilization <85% for ≥1 calendar month between Jun 1-Oct 31 2026

AMBER.reasoning: <85% Asian refinery utilization is a demand-destruction threshold, regime-shift framed.

BLUE.reasoning: East Asia refinery utilization <85% for a month overstates demand destruction; strategic buyers, SPR releases, and Russia/Atlantic basin flows absorb the shock.

GRAY.reasoning: E. Asia refinery utilization is clean and sharp.

RED.reasoning: East Asian refinery utilization <85% from demand destruction captures downstream coordination failure. OK.

VIOLET.reasoning: Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF 4-12 month Hormuz closure occurs, THEN global oil prices reach USD 110-135/barrel within 30 days of closure declaration
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: WTI or Brent crude daily close >=USD 110/barrel on >=3 trading days within 30 days of the first official or operationally confirmed Hormuz closure date (as reported by IEA or CENTCOM).

AMBER.reasoning: Price threshold with discrete trading-day count is a good observable for a closure-regime event.

BLUE.reasoning: Oil price spike on 4-12 mo closure is market-clearing response. BLUE-neutral technical prediction.

RED.reasoning: Hormuz closure → $110-135/bbl is UNDERESTIMATE. Historical shock math: 20% global oil supply disruption in 4-12 month window pushes prices to $150-200+. RED tail is much fatter.

VIOLET.reasoning: Oil price response to Hormuz closure depends on specific decisions by Saudi Aramco (MBS), US SPR release policy (Trump/DOE), and OPEC+ coordination. Past strait-disruption price responses have been capped by Saudi discretionary capacity. $110-135 band may be too high if MBS opens taps.
VIOLET.modified_suggestion: Consider widening to $95-135; MBS has demonstrated willingness to flood market when politically convenient for Trump.

GRAY.reasoning: Oil price threshold is clean; 30-day window well-bounded.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF 4-12 month closure, THEN global LNG import disruption forces 2-3 major importers (Japan, Korea, EU) to activate emergency reserves by month 3 of closure
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: Official announcements from Japan METI, Korea MOTIE, or EU energy ministry confirming emergency LNG/energy reserve drawdown by month 3 of Hormuz closure

AMBER.reasoning: Emergency reserve activation is a discrete institutional regime signal.

BLUE.reasoning: Emergency reserve activation is institutional response — BLUE-compatible (institutions work as designed).

RED.reasoning: By month 3 is conservative — RED view: LNG reserves activated within 30-60 days given Japan/Korea's <3-month stockpiles. Also, ALL three importers likely activate, not 2-3.
RED.modified_suggestion: Tighten to month 2; require all 3 of Japan/Korea/EU.

VIOLET.reasoning: LNG emergency reserves are ministerial decisions by named actors (METI, MOTIE) — agency-grounded.

GRAY.reasoning: Government reserve drawdown announcements are observable.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H3 (4-12 month closure), THEN geopolitical realignment crystallizes through structural energy-security pressure: ≥3 nations announce forward energy-security partnerships (India-Middle East energy deals, European liquefied LNG diversification agreements, US regional basing agreements). While Hormuz is operationally closed, diplomatic/commercial architecture shifts to assume straits closure as existential risk; institutions harden assumption into permanent policy.
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: During or shortly after any declared/de facto Hormuz closure period (2026-2027), track major energy-security partnerships announced: ≥3 new bilateral/multilateral energy agreements with structural resilience components (LNG supply diversification, long-term contracts, strategic reserve builds). Agreements explicitly reference Hormuz closure vulnerability.

AMBER.reasoning: Geopolitical realignment via forward energy-security partnerships is a structural regime-shift indicator. Well-specified.

BLUE.reasoning: Energy-security partnerships are institutional realignment BLUE predicts under systemic shock. Reasonable.

RED.reasoning: Energy-security hardening is RED-aligned geopolitical lock-in.

VIOLET.reasoning: Energy-security partnerships are literally named-head-of-state agreements — agency-grounded.

GRAY.reasoning: 'Agreements explicitly reference Hormuz closure vulnerability' is a linguistic test in diplomatic text — too brittle; agreements may be structurally responsive without naming the strait.
GRAY.modified_suggestion: Drop the explicit-reference requirement; accept structural resilience components (diversification, reserve builds) within 90 days of closure.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF >12mo Hormuz closure, THEN Khamenei/IRGC sustain closure; Brent crude averages >=120 USD/bbl and Strait remains de facto closed for cumulative >12 months 2026-2028.
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Strait of Hormuz remains functionally impassable for cumulative >12 months 2026-2028. Brent crude averages >=120 USD/bbl. UN or shipping authority documents closure.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF 4-12 month closure, THEN LNG spot prices in NW Europe exceed $35/MMBtu on ≥3 separate trading days by 2026-09-30
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Bloomberg or Platts shows ≥3 daily NW Europe LNG prices >$35/MMBtu between Apr 18-Sep 30 2026

$35/MMBtu on 3+ days overstates breakdown; EU gas storage policy, reshuffled LNG cargoes, and demand response are robust absorption mechanisms that cap spikes.

LNG spot >$35/MMBtu on 3+ days under 4-12mo Hormuz closure is a clean energy-shock indicator. RED-aligned.
LNG price threshold falsifiable.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H3, THEN insurance premium for VLCCs on Cape route exceeds 3.0% of hull value by 2026-08-15
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: ICS or Lloyd's published VLCC war risk premium >3.0% on at least one publication date between May 1-Aug 15 2026

criticVerdicts.reasoning: >3% hull-value war-risk premium is a threshold-crossing insurance-regime signal.

3.0% hull-value premium is plausibly high but under-specifies whether the Cape rerouting itself clears the risk. Reframe to include that premiums settle at a lower equilibrium once Cape rerouting is underwritten as standard.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: IF H3, THEN VLCC war-risk premium spikes above 3.0% of hull value at least once between May 1-Aug 15 2026, then settles into a stable Cape-route premium of 1.0-2.0% by 2026-09-30 as underwriters normalize the reroute.

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.
VLCC war-risk premium >3.0% hull value is a sharp brittleness signal. Good.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H3 (4-12 month closure), THEN ≥1 OPEC+ member outside Gulf reduces crude output by ≥2 Mb/d cumulatively over closure window due to sanctions/credit constraints cascading from energy price volatility
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: IEA or EIA monthly data shows ≥2 Mb/d aggregate reduction from Feb 2026 baseline for named non-Gulf OPEC+ cohort (Angola, Iraq, Nigeria, Russia, or Kazakhstan) with causal attribution to financial or sanctions pressure by Dec 31 2026

criticVerdicts.reasoning: 2 Mb/d non-Gulf OPEC+ cut from sanctions/credit cascade is a clear cross-regime contagion signal.
2 Mb/d non-Gulf OPEC+ reduction from financial contagion overstates cascade risk; OPEC+ coordination and Gulf spare capacity are the canonical absorption mechanisms.
Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.
Non-Gulf OPEC+ member output reduction via sanctions/credit contagion is a clean second-order channel. Good.
'Causal attribution to financial pressure' is hard to establish; reframe.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: IF H3, THEN 2+ Mb/d aggregate output reduction from named non-Gulf OPEC+ states during Jun-Dec 2026, with 2+ peer-reviewed or IEA analyses citing financial-sanction mechanism.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H3, THEN structural refinery utilization in East Asia (China, India, Japan, South Korea combined) declines to <85% for ≥1 month by Oct 2026 due to demand destruction from elevated product prices
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: IEA or joint national statistical agency data shows combined refinery utilization <85% for ≥1 calendar month between Jun 1-Oct 31 2026

criticVerdicts.reasoning: <85% Asian refinery utilization is a demand-destruction threshold, regime-shift framed.
East Asia refinery utilization <85% for a month overstates demand destruction; strategic buyers, SPR releases, and Russia/Atlantic basin flows absorb the shock.
Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.
